Level 1 completed! Now on Level 2. Let me analyze the new board.
Looking at the image, I see:
1. A large purple L-shape in the upper right
2. A small cluster of colored squares on the left (orange, sky blue, yellow, green)
3. A red cross on the right
4. Four containers at the bottom with U-shapes: orange, sky blue, yellow, green
This looks similar to Level 1 but with more colors. The goal is likely the same: grow bars (controlled by U-shapes) until the red dots reach the red crosses.
Let me inspect the segmentation to understand the layout better.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Analyze Level 2
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Objects by color:")
from collections import defaultdict
by_color = defaultdict(list)
for n in seg['nodes']:
    by_color[n['color']].append(n)

for color, nodes in sorted(by_color.items()):
    for n in nodes:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {color}: {n['pixels']}px, rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
